findings.title: verifier.started emitted after sandbox.run() — event ordering inverted
findings.detail: In src/agents/verifier/index.ts, verifier.started was emitted only after this.sandbox.run(input) resolved, meaning the started event always fired after passed/failed/timeout. Any trace consumer checking event ordering would receive an impossible sequence. VerifierController (controller.ts) was correct; the standalone VerifierAgent class was broken.
findings.fix_sketch: Move emitEvent('verifier.started') before this.sandbox.run(input). Generate runId with randomUUID() before the call.

findings.title: mergeInvariantFailures treats 'partial' sandbox result as passed even when invariant failures exist
findings.detail: In src/agents/verifier/controller.ts mergeInvariantFailures(), the status flip only checked status === 'passed'. If sandboxResult.status was 'partial' (no test script, green-with-banner), invariant failures were silently dropped and the merged result remained 'partial', which statusToEventKind() maps to verifier.passed. A PR with invariant violations would be opened as if verified.
findings.fix_sketch: Check both 'passed' and 'partial': if ((status === 'passed' || status === 'partial') && invariantFailures.length > 0) merged.status = 'failed'

findings.title: cloneFromSha passes unvalidated repoUrl directly to git clone — RCE via argument injection
findings.detail: In src/agents/verifier/sandbox.ts:cloneFromSha(), the repoUrl parameter was passed verbatim to ['clone', '--quiet', repoUrl, tempDir] with no validation. A URL starting with '-' would be interpreted as a git option. A non-https:// URL (file://, git://, etc.) could access local paths or bypass network controls. Similarly, sha was passed to git checkout without a '--' separator, allowing a sha value of '-q' or similar to inject git options.
findings.fix_sketch: Add SAFE_REPO_URL_RE = /^https:\/\/github\.com\/[\w.-]+\/[\w.-]+(\.git)?$/ validation; add SAFE_SHA_RE = /^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$/i validation; add '--' separator in both git clone and git checkout argv arrays.

findings.title: Stored XSS in dashboard: GitHub PR/commit data injected unescaped into innerHTML
findings.detail: In dashboard/index.html, fetchPRs() and fetchCommits() built innerHTML by interpolating pr.title, pr.head.ref, pr.base.ref, pr.html_url, label names, and commit messages (c.commit.message, c.html_url) without HTML escaping. A PR title or branch name containing executes script in the operator's browser. The page also stores a GitHub PAT in localStorage, making token exfiltration trivial. Local-SQLite renderers in the same file correctly used the existing esc() helper; only the GitHub-fetch renderers were vulnerable.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap every interpolated GitHub field in esc(): pr.title, pr.html_url, pr.head.ref, pr.base.ref, l.name, c.commit.message, c.html_url, c.sha.

findings.title: Codex worker spawns with full process.env — leaks all secrets to subprocess
findings.detail: In src/workers/codex.ts, createCodexAdapter never sets `env` on runStreaming, so spawn-runner falls back to process.env. The codex CLI receives GITHUB_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, IFLEET_HMAC_SECRET, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. The claude adapter deliberately scrubs these via claudeChildEnv(); codex has no equivalent.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a codex env allowlist mirroring claudeChildEnv (only CODEX/OPENAI keys + PATH/HOME) and pass it as `env:` to runStreaming. Never pass raw process.env.

findings.title: Codex finalize hardcodes ok:true even on rate-limit/error events — silent task loss
findings.detail: In src/workers/codex.ts, the finalize function always returns { ok: true } and provides no classifyExit. A zero exit that emitted rate_limit/error events is reported as success with no rateLimited flag, so rate-limited codex tasks are treated as completed rather than re-queued — violating mandatory rule 5 (exit 2/3 → cancel/blocked).
findings.fix_sketch: Track rate_limit/error events in closure state; derive ok from whether a terminal error was seen; add classifyExit mirroring claude.ts.

findings.title: Default-allow author policy: empty allowedAuthors returns true — any GitHub user triggers auto-permission worker
findings.detail: In src/queue/github.ts:68-71, isAuthorAllowed returns true when repo.allowedAuthors is undefined/empty (only a construction-time warning is logged). On a public branch-protected repo, any user can open an auto:ship issue whose body is fed verbatim to a worker running with auto/acceptEdits permissions.
findings.fix_sketch: Fail closed: when allowedAuthors is empty/undefined return false, or require an explicit allowAnyAuthor:true opt-in in RepoRef.

findings.title: Architecture-rule violation: handleForcePr calls octokit and git push directly from orchestrator
findings.detail: CLAUDE.md load-bearing rule: SprintManager emits events, never calls GitHub directly. handleForcePr in src/orchestrator/handlers/pr-decisions.ts both shells `git push -u origin` and calls octokit.rest.pulls.create directly inside the orchestrator handler tree. Also hardcodes base:'main' ignoring per-repo defaultBranch.
findings.fix_sketch: Route the force-PR push + PR creation through a GitHubQueue.forcePr bridge method instead of importing Octokit into the orchestrator handler.

findings.title: BUDGET_USD guard is dead — totalCostUsd never propagates through pipeline bridge
findings.detail: SprintManager accumulates spend from result.totalCostUsd and calls checkBudget, but mapPipelineResult in src/orchestrator/pipeline-bridge.ts never sets SpawnResult.totalCostUsd because PipelineResult carries no totalCostUsd (only totalTokens is propagated). accumulateCost always adds 0, so the budget cap can never fire on the real pipeline path.
findings.fix_sketch: Add totalCostUsd to PipelineResult (sum of attempt SpawnResult.totalCostUsd) and propagate it in mapPipelineResult.

findings.title: No authentication on Langfuse nginx vhost — all LLM traces publicly exposed
findings.detail: nginx/langfuse.conf proxies location / straight to the Langfuse web container with no IP allowlist or auth. Langfuse self-host has open sign-up by default, so anyone reaching the host can register and read every captured trace (prompts, code, secrets in payloads).
findings.fix_sketch: Add allow/deny IP allowlist or basic auth on location /, and set AUTH_DISABLE_SIGNUP=true in the langfuse env.

findings.title: Discord webhook URL (with secret token) persisted into SQLite outbox
findings.detail: broadcastIFleet in src/observability/discord-broadcast.ts enqueues JSON.stringify({content,url}) where url is the full DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK URL containing the secret token. This writes the credential into the durable SQLite outbox at rest and into deadLetterEntries() output operators inspect.
findings.fix_sketch: Store only channel sentinel + content; have the drain resolve the webhook URL from env at send time. Redact url in deadLetterEntries.

findings.title: broadcastIFleet outbox double-sends on fast-path success
findings.detail: When an outbox is wired, every broadcast is both enqueued AND fast-path HTTP sent. On fast-path success markSent runs, but the row is also visible to the drain job, and on fast-path failure the row stays pending so drainOnce re-sends it. Same Discord message delivered twice with no coordination between the in-process timer and the SQLite drain cursor.
findings.fix_sketch: Pick one delivery path: enqueue-only with drain owning all sends, or mark the row 'sending' before the fast-path attempt so drainOnce skips in-flight rows.

findings.title: Verify subprocesses inherit full process.env including GITHUB_TOKEN and HMAC secret
findings.detail: runProcess in src/verify/spawn-util.ts defaults env to process.env, and ci.ts/playwright.ts/screenshot.ts spawn pnpm/npx against an untrusted worktree without scrubbing. Worktree package.json scripts execute with the full parent env, giving worker-authored code a secret-exfiltration path during verify.
findings.fix_sketch: Pass an explicit minimal env allowlist from the verify callers; never default to process.env for code-running subprocesses against untrusted worktrees.

findings.title: recoverStale attempt-cap off-by-one leaves tasks with attempts == maxAttempts-1 stuck forever
findings.detail: In src/queue/store.ts:344-373, the failed branch uses attempts >= maxAttempts and the recovered branch uses attempts+1 < maxAttempts. A row with attempts == maxAttempts-1 matches neither branch and is left in_flight forever.
findings.fix_sketch: Make predicates exhaustive: failed = attempts+1 >= maxAttempts, recovered = attempts+1 < maxAttempts.

findings.title: task.mode silently dropped on store round-trip — never persisted
findings.detail: In src/queue/store.ts, QueuedTask carries an optional mode field but TaskStore.insert never writes it and rowToTask never reads it. A task created with mode set loses it on any store round-trip, defaulting downstream to standard routing — silent regression for ralph/ulw/tdd/deslop tasks.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a mode column (ALTER migration) and persist/read task.mode, or fold mode into routing_hints JSON.

findings.title: Sprint completion gates on global running.size not per-sprint — cross-sprint interference
findings.detail: In src/orchestrator/sprint.ts tick() the sprint-completion gate requires this.running.size === 0 (no task across ANY sprint). With cross-task parallelism in scope, a sprint whose own tasks are all terminal won't transition to completed while an unrelated sprint still has a running task.
findings.fix_sketch: Gate on a per-sprint running count (some running entry with matching sprintId) instead of the global running map size.

findings.title: onCancel/onStop record deliberate cancels as 'failed' — corrupts analytics
findings.detail: In src/orchestrator/handlers/control-plane.ts:146-147, onCancel and onStop both call store.updateState(id,'failed'). A user-initiated /cancel or /stop is recorded as a failure, corrupting success/failure analytics and potentially misleading PR-rejection learning that keys off failed vs cancelled.
findings.fix_sketch: Introduce a 'cancelled' unified state (or metadata flag) for operator-cancel; keep 'failed' only for genuine pipeline failures.

findings.title: Command injection via --base flag in validate-claims git diff
findings.detail: In scripts/validate-claims.ts:591-608, changedMarkdownFiles builds a shell string `git diff --name-only ${base}...HEAD` run through execSync, where base comes from the --base CLI flag. A value like 'main; rm -rf x' or '$(...)' is interpreted by the shell.
findings.fix_sketch: Use execFileSync('git', ['diff','--name-only',`${base}...HEAD`,'--','*.md']) so base is an argv element, not shell-interpolated.

findings.title: Husky pre-push runs npm instead of pnpm — diverges from CI and will fail on pnpm-only repo
findings.detail: .husky/pre-push runs npm run typecheck/lint/test, but the repo declares packageManager pnpm@10.33.2, CI uses pnpm --frozen-lockfile, and there is no package-lock.json. The local gate diverges from CI.
findings.fix_sketch: Switch the hook to pnpm typecheck/lint/test to match package.json and CI.
